name: Affiliate Program: Join, Status, Payout, Leaderboard
description: Customer-recruits-customer referral program (Affiliate 10% -> Partner 15% -> Reseller 20% -> Strategic 25% commission tiers) with join form, status/earnings dashboard, payout requests, and a public leaderboard, embedded on the homepage.

frontend.evidence: FIXED 2026-07-09. The shared p4Api() envelope-unwrap bug (root cause shared with CAP-CRM-001/005, workers/src/lib/response.js's {success,data,error,timestamp} shape) is fixed once for all four call sites. Field-name/logic fixes per call site: handleJoin's real response has no affiliate key ({registered,ref_code,referral_url,tier,commission,dashboard_url} or {already_registered,ref_code,tier}) — p4JoinAffiliate() now checks d.success && (d.registered||d.already_registered) and sends only {name,email} (handleJoin never reads user_id/company/affiliate_type from the body at all; it derives identity from authCtx.userId or the email itself, and always starts new joins at the base AFFILIATE tier server-side). handleGetStatus requires a real authCtx.userId (401 without one) and never reads a ?user_id= query param — closed deliberately during an anonymous-exposure/IDOR fix (anyone who knew/guessed an affiliate's email could previously pull their stats). p4LoadAffStatus()/p4RequestPayout() now gate on a real client-side session-token check (p4HasSession(), the same cdb_token p4Api() already attaches as the Authorization header) before calling, so a signed-out visitor gets an honest 'sign in' message instead of a silent/misleading failure, and no doomed network call fires. p4RenderAffStatus() itself had further, previously-uncatalogued field-mapping bugs against the real handleGetStatus shape (aff.commission_rate/aff.total_referrals/aff.total_earned_inr/aff.pending_payout_inr never existed at any level of a real response) — fixed to tier_details.commission_pct and stats.{total_referrals,conversions,total_commission_earned_inr,pending_payout_inr}. handleGetLeaderboard deliberately never returns a per-affiliate earnings figure (privacy) — the leaderboard's fabricated always-₹0 'earnings' line was removed and replaced with the real (previously unused) badge emoji field. Playwright-verified end-to-end for all four flows, including the signed-out/signed-in session split.

navigation.evidence: Reached via a same-page anchor nav tab (href="#affiliate-hub") at frontend/index.html:1215,7066,20300. Separately, frontend/sitemap.html:222 links a bare /affiliate-hub path (without the /# prefix workers/test/deadEndLinks.test.mjs locks in as correct elsewhere in this same file) — not live-tested in this pass, flagged as an unresolved loose end rather than asserted broken or working.

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/referralAttribution.test.mjs and workers/test/anonymousExposureAudit.test.mjs unchanged, still cover the handlers in isolation. The frontend call sites (all four) were verified this pass via an ad-hoc Playwright click-through (not committed to the repo) rather than a permanent regression test — a real gap, flagged as a follow-up.

verification.evidence: workers/src/handlers/affiliateSystem.js re-read (handleJoin/handleGetStatus/handleGetLeaderboard/handleRequestPayout real response shapes, TIERS.commission_pct units). Playwright click-through of the real frontend/index.html confirmed: join sends the real field names and renders the status panel; a signed-out status/payout attempt fails fast with an honest message and zero network call; an authenticated status fetch (mocked response shaped exactly to handleGetStatus) renders real nested stats (referrals, 10% commission, real earned amount); payout succeeds and no longer sends a fake user_id; the leaderboard renders the real badge field with no fabricated earnings. Full backend suite (188 files/2012 tests) green — this was a frontend-only change. NOT YET verified against the live deployed backend — customer_journey_complete stays false pending a post-merge production smoke test with a real account.
verification.residual_limitation: By design, not a wiring bug: a non-customer external affiliate who joins via email only (handleJoin's public, no-platform-account path) currently has no self-serve way to check status or request payout in-page, because handleGetStatus/handleRequestPayout correctly require a real platform session (the old ?user_id= lookup was closed as an IDOR fix and is not reverted here). Tracked as a follow-up product decision (e.g. a private per-affiliate lookup token), documented in frontend/index.html's own comment above the affiliate-hub section.

notes: A second, independent revenue-program journey (alongside sales-crm's CAP-CRM-001/005) fixed together with the shared root cause (frontend/index.html's p4Api() envelope unwrap) plus four program-specific defects on top (see frontend.evidence). MSSP/reseller white-label partnering remains a genuinely separate backend system (D1-based, /api/partners/* prefix) from this KV-based /api/affiliate/* program — see docs/capability-registry/domains/mssp.json, not duplicated here despite overlapping tier-name vocabulary.

name: Outbound Sponsor/Affiliate Click Tracking
description: Fire-and-forget click tracking for outbound sponsor links (Hack The Box, TryHackMe, Udemy, NordVPN) shown on the homepage, plus an aggregate stats view — a separate, unrelated system from CAP-AFF-001's inbound referral program despite the shared word 'affiliate'.

frontend.evidence: frontend/index.html:8263-8270 (trackAffiliate()) fires a non-blocking POST to /api/affiliate/click with {program, link_id, ...} on outbound sponsor-link clicks, matching workers/src/index.js:4447-4460's handler, which inserts into a dedicated affiliate_clicks D1 table and always returns 204 regardless of outcome (by design, per its own comment) — no response-body parsing on the frontend side, so the envelope-unwrap defect affecting CAP-AFF-001 does not apply here. Verified 2026-07-08.

navigation.evidence: The sponsor links this tracks are embedded directly in frontend/index.html's homepage content.

test_coverage.evidence: This is an inline route in workers/src/index.js, not a separate handler file; no test file targets /api/affiliate/click or handleAffiliateStats directly.

notes: Small, working, low-risk capability (fire-and-forget analytics, no customer-visible success/failure state to get wrong). P6 reflects the absence of any test, not a known defect. Naming collision with CAP-AFF-001 (both use the word 'affiliate' under the same /api/affiliate/ prefix) is worth a future rename for clarity, but is not itself a functional bug.
